name: Old Fashioned Apple Cider Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Divide the pastry almost in half and roll out the larger half on a lightly floured surface to form a 13-inch circle.
2. Line a 9-inch pie tin with the pastry, trimming the edge 1/2-inch beyond the rim of the pie tin.
3. Combine apples, 1 cup apple cider or juice, and sugar in a 3-quart saucepan. Cook over high heat until the mixture boils.
4. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 8 minutes or until apples are tender. Drain apples, reserving syrup.
5. Add enough additional apple cider to the syrup to make 1 1/3 cups. Return syrup and apples to the saucepan.
6. Combine cornstarch and water in a small bowl and stir until well blended. Stir cornstarch mixture and cinnamon into the apple mixture.
7. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture boils. Remove from heat and stir in butter.
8. Pour filling into pastry-lined pie tin.
9. Roll out remaining pastry to an 11-inch circle.
10. Fold pastry gently into quarters, cutting slits in folds.
11. Gently unfold pastry on top of filling, trimming edge 1 inch beyond the rim of the pie tin.
12. Fold top crust under lower crust and form a ridge by fluting the edge of the pie.
13. Bake at 400°F for 40-45 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on wire rack before cutting and serving.
